titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.12

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=664 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:39:23.03292+00 | translation moa=665 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:39:21.073643+00

div.p:
. D . dei Gratia Rex Scot’ . Episcopis Abbatibus . Comitibus . Iusticiis . Baronibus . Vicecomitibus . prepositis . Ministris . et Omnibus .
fidelibus suis francis . et Anglicis . Clericis . et Laicis . tam futuris . quam presentibus totius terrę suę . salutem . Sciatis me dedisse . et concessisse .
deo et ecclesię sanctę Marie de Hadintune . Clerchetune . in perpetuam elemosinam . cum suis rectis diuisis ex utraque parte Aquę sicut . Willelmus .
de Graham . et Durandus vicecomes . et Ricardus clericus . et Osbertus prior . de Edeneb’ . et Malbet de Libertune . et Gilandr’ filius Oggu . preierunt . et circuerunt .
postquam Thoroldus Archidiaconus mecum finiuit apud pebles . et etiam sicut antea mensurata fuit . Concedo predictę ecclesię .
unum plenarium . Toft[um] . iuxta ecclesiam . in villa de Hadintun’ . et Omnes decimas . et rectitudines ecclesiasticas de tota Ha
dintuneschire . tam de Molendinis quam de aliis rebus . Volo itaque et firmiter precipio quod predicta ecclesia teneat . et habeat . prenominatam
terram . Clerchetun’ . solam . et quietam . de me . et de Theino . et de omnibus aliis qui terram predictam tenuerint . de Hadintun . de me . et heredibus meis .
et etiam de heredibus eorum . et etiam omnes alias rectitudines . et consuetudines . in decimis . et elemosinis . ita libere . et honorifice . et
et quiete . et plenarie . sicut aliqua elemosina in tota terra mea . melius . et plenius . et honorificentius . et quiecius . potest . dari .
et concedi . Has autem omnes prenominatas elemosinas . do . et Carta mea confirmo . pro anima mea . et patris . matrisque meę .
et animabus antecessorum . et successorum meorum . Annuente et concedente hoc . Henrico filio meo . et hoc idem testante :
Roberto Episcopo Sancte Andreę . et Iohanne Episcopo . de Glesgu . et Gregorio Episcopo de Duncheldin . et Galfrido Abbate de Dunfermelin
et Dionisio priore de Scon’ . et Rogero priore de Dunfermelin . et Roberto de Sigillo . et Dunecano Comite . et Hugone de Moruilla . et
et Mallise Comite . et Edwardo Cunestabulario . et Leod de Brechin . et Rand’ de sules . et Rogero nepote Episcopi Roberti . Apud . pert .
xviii . kalendas . IVLII .

David, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, justices, barons, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all his sworn men, french and English, clerk and lay, as well future as present, of his entire land, greeting. Know me to have given and conceded, to God and the church of St Mary at Haddington, Clerkington in perpetual alms, with its right marches on either side of the water, just as William de Graham, Durand the sheriff, Richard the clerk, Osbert the prior of Edinburgh, Mael Bethad of Liberton, and Gillanders son of Oggu, led the way and went around; afterwards, Thorald the archdeacon set the bounds with me at Peebles, even also as it was measured earlier. I concede to the aforesaid church one full toft near the church in the toun of Haddington, and all teinds and ecclesiastical revenues from the whole of Haddingtonshire, as much from mills as from other properties. I will and strictly command that the aforesaid church may hold and have the aforenamed land of Clerkington alone, and quit of me and of the thane, and of all others who may have held the aforesaid land of Haddington; of me and my heirs, and also of their heirs; and also all other revenues and customs, in teinds and alms, so freely, honourably, quitly, and fully as any alms in my entire land can best, most fully, honourably, and quitly be given and conceded. All these aforenamed alms I give, and by my charter make firm, for the sake of my soul, and of my father's, and my mother's, and the souls of my predecessors and successors. Henry my son agrees and consents. And witnessing this same matter: Robert, bishop of St Andrews; John, bishop of Glasgow; Gregory, bishop of Dunkeld; Geoffrey, abbot of Dunfermline; Denis, prior of Scone; Roger, prior of Dunfermline; Robert de Sigillo; Duncan the earl; Hugh de Morville; Malise the earl; Edward the constable; Leod of Brechin; Ranulf de Soulis; Roger, Bishop Robert's nephew. At Perth on the 18th kalends of July [14 June].
